title: What I want my grandchildren to inherit
subtitle: A journal set for writing out what the new family money legacy actually looks like.
excerpt: A journal prompt set for naming the legacy you are building. Five prompts, thirty minutes, drawn from Day 14 of Rebecca's MONEY program. Use when the future you are creating needs to become specific rather than abstract.
sourceNotes: Drawn from Day 14 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and Week 2 of The Money Journal (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use after completing the Day 14 tapping or ritual, when you want to write the new family story in specific detail.

content.text: A journal set for Day 14 of the MONEY program: the day for naming what you are building for the people who come after you. Five prompts, around thirty minutes.
New to journal prompts as a practice? Read
How do I feel about the week of work just completed?
Write for five minutes. Name what shifted, what surprised you, what still feels uncertain.
What has already changed in how I think about money?
Name two or three concrete differences — in thought, in behaviour, or in how you respond.
What do I want my grandchildren to inherit from me around money?
Not just money itself — the beliefs, the habits, the relationship with earning and keeping. Be specific.
What is one thing I will do differently this week to build that legacy?
Concrete action, not intention. Something that can be done before the next prompt.
What belief do I want to carry forward into Week 3?
Write one sentence. Finish this: "I now choose to believe..."
Drawn from Day 14 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025) and the Week 2 Reflection in The Money Journal: 12 Weeks to Peace, Freedom & Overflow (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
